rationale: Reduce Windows CLI argument-length risk by enforcing file-based inputs and tight inline limits without touching other files.

edits.content: {"schema":"brainflow.cli_arglen_policy.v1","max_inline_chars":2000,"prefer_file_inputs":true,"force_file_inputs_over_chars":4000,"notes":"Use temp files for prompts/output to avoid Windows command-line length limits."}

risks: Unknown consumer may ignore new policy file if not wired.
expected_benefit: Lower chance of CLI failures on long prompts by standardizing file-based inputs.
